hints.Ne: 'Ne' is a negative particle introducing a prohibition
hints.Zacharia: 'Zacharia' is a proper noun in the vocative, the name Zechariah
hints.dixit: 'Dixit' means 'he said' but does not appear in this phrase

steps.reveal: timeas = you may fear / do not fear

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 2nd person would be 'times'; 'timeas' has subjunctive -ea-

hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are the principal parts of 'teneo' (to hold)
hints.taceo, tacere, tacui, tacitum: These are the principal parts of 'taceo' (to be silent)
hints.habeo, habere, habui, habitum: These are the principal parts of 'habeo' (to have)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Zacharias'; the -a ending here signals vocative of a 1st declension masculine
hints.ablative: While -a can be ablative for 1st declension, context shows Zechariah is being addressed

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'Zacharias' follows the Greek -as pattern of 1st declension
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'Zacharias' follows 1st declension
hints.indeclinable: 'Zacharias' does decline: Zacharias, Zachariae, Zachariae, Zachariam, Zacharia
